food_name: Banana Fritters with Chocolate and Cheese
aliases: Banana Fritters with Chocolate and Cheese

english: Banana Fritters with Chocolate and Cheese
Fried Banana with Chocolate and Cheese

food_subgroup: Fried banana snacks with sweet toppings

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general banana fritter entry (FOODON_03302757); the chocolate and cheese topping variant is not separately coded. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers fried banana / banana fritter as a closest parent; the chocolate-cheese topping combination is not separately enumerated in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for banana fritters with chocolate and cheese toppings.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of banana fritters with chocolate and cheese is approximately 150 g, comprising 3 to 4 battered and deep-fried banana pieces drizzled with chocolate sauce and topped with grated processed cheese.

nutrient_source.title: Pisang Goreng (Fried Banana) — component-based estimation using USDA FoodData Central entries for fried banana fritter batter, banana, chocolate sauce, and processed cheese
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (Foundation Foods and SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Banana Fritters with Chocolate and Cheese exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central data for: (1) banana fritter / fried banana batter (NDB 11920 analogue), (2) ripe banana (FDC ID 1105314), (3) chocolate syrup/sauce (FDC ID 169652), and (4) processed American-style cheese (FDC ID 1098457), weighted to reflect a typical Indonesian pisang goreng with chocolate and cheese topping at approximately 55% fried banana batter, 20% banana flesh, 15% chocolate sauce, and 10% processed cheese by weight. Energy and macros are cross-checked against published Indonesian street-food composition data in FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table for South East Asia (2nd ed.).

gi_source.title: Banana, ripe, fried — GI analogous estimate based on 'Banana, ripe' (GI 51) and 'Banana fritter' (GI 65) entries; chocolate sauce contribution raises effective GI to mid-range
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database lists fried banana / banana fritter at GI approximately 65 (medium). Ripe banana alone is GI 51 (low-medium). The addition of chocolate sauce (high sugar, GI ~60–65) and processed cheese (negligible GI contribution) supports a composite GI estimate of 65 for this dish. Glycemic load per 150 g serving = (65 × 38.5 × 1.50) / 100 = 37.5 g GL, classified as high GL per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from processed cheese and chocolate sauce
hypertension.guidance: Each 150 g serving provides approximately 218 mg sodium; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and avoid additional high-sodium condiments, as processed cheese contributes the majority of sodium in this dish.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load and elevated sugar content from chocolate sauce and ripe banana
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic index of 65 and a glycemic load of approximately 37.5 per standard serving, banana fritters with chocolate and cheese can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation and should be consumed only occasionally and in small portions by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from deep-frying oil and processed cheese, combined with added sugars from chocolate sauce
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each 150 g serving provides approximately 6.3 g saturated fat and 27 g sugar; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this deep-fried, high-sugar snack and consider lower-fat preparation alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Banana Fritters with Chocolate and Cheese (Indonesian: Pisang Goreng Coklat Keju) is a popular Indonesian street-food snack consisting of ripe bananas coated in a wheat-flour batter and deep-fried until golden and crispy. The fritters are then generously drizzled with sweet chocolate sauce and topped with grated or shredded processed cheese, creating a distinctive sweet-savoury flavour combination that is widely enjoyed across Indonesia and in Indonesian food stalls throughout Singapore and Southeast Asia. The dish is a modern, indulgent variation of the classic pisang goreng (plain fried banana), elevated with confectionery toppings that are especially popular among younger consumers. Texturally, the exterior is crisp and lightly chewy from the batter, while the interior remains soft and sweet from the ripe banana. The chocolate sauce adds richness and sweetness, while the processed cheese contributes a mild salty, creamy counterpoint. Nutritionally, this snack is energy-dense at approximately 268 kcal per 100 g, with a moderate fat content of 11.5 g and a high carbohydrate content of 38.5 g, of which 18 g is sugar. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), but the glycemic load per standard serving of 150 g is high at approximately 37.5, making this snack unsuitable for frequent consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. The dish is vegetarian and typically halal. It is commonly sold at Indonesian martabak and pisang goreng stalls, including Smile Martabak outlets in Singapore.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Banana Fritters with Chocolate and Cheese was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Nutrient values are estimated by component-based calculation using USDA FoodData Central data for fried banana fritter, ripe banana, chocolate sauce, and processed cheese, weighted by typical recipe proportions. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for fried banana fritter (GI 65). Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-based estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central (Foundation Foods and SR Legacy) as no exact approved-source entry exists for this specific dish. Values should be verified against a laboratory analysis or a future approved regional database entry if one becomes available. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA component sources used for this estimation. 3. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA component sources used for this estimation. 4. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat are estimated from the frying oil (palm oil) and cheese components using USDA SR Legacy data. 5. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for fried banana fritter (GI 65); no direct GI measurement for this specific chocolate-cheese variant was found. 6. The food_group has been reclassified from the input value of 'Dairy and eggs' to 'Snacks', which more accurately reflects the nature of this deep-fried street-food snack. 7. The dish is served at Smile Martabak - Funan (field survey data) at SGD 6.90. 8. Halal status is assumed based on typical Indonesian Muslim food preparation practices at martabak stalls; formal halal certification was not verified from the source data.
